query: During the specified time range of March 9, 2021, from 18:00 to 18:30, a failure was detected within the system. The exact time of the root cause occurrence is unknown, which complicates the diagnosis process. Please pinpoint the root cause occurrence datetime.
answer: {"root cause occurrence datetime": "2021-03-09 18:08:00", "root cause component": "Redis01", "root cause reason": "Analysis of the full dependency graph points to Redis01 as Patient Zero driving the cascading failure across this stack. Key evidence: (1) Redis01 shows extreme saturation metrics \u2014 total_commands_processed at ~5.02B, keyspace_hits at ~3.82B, total_connections_received at ~32M, and rdb_changes_since_last_save at ~1.11B \u2014 all significantly higher than Redis02, indicating Redis01 is absorbing a disproportionate and unsustainable command load. (2) Redis01's used_memory_peak is only ~50MB versus Redis02's ~95MB, which is anomalously low and suggests Redis01 may have evicted or flushed data (possibly due to maxmemory policy triggering at the 4GB cap), causing cache misses that pushed load downstream to MySQL and Tomcat tiers. (3) The network packet counts on Redis01 (NETPacketsIn ~5.68B, NETPacketsOut ~5.16B) are roughly 14x higher than IG01/IG02, confirming Redis01 is under extreme network-level pressure consistent with a thundering herd or connection storm. (4) All four Tomcat instances (Tomcat01\u201304) are red with elevated JVM memory usage, high ProcessingTime, and high MaxTime (~324\u2013330 seconds), consistent with threads blocking on slow or failed cache lookups being redirected to MySQL \u2014 MySQL01 slow logs confirm queries from Tomcat01/IPAddress with query times up to 730ms. (5) Mysql02 shows an InnoDB page_cleaner warning (1000ms loop taking 5440ms) at 01:55, indicating write pressure spillover from cache misses. (6) MG01 and MG02 (middleware gateway) are red with high NoHeap usage (506MB and 947MB respectively), consistent with connection pool exhaustion from retrying against a degraded Redis01. (7) IG01 and IG02 are red and acting as ingress, routing all traffic through the stack \u2014 their relative health (low KB/s network) confirms they are not the origin. (8) apache01 filesystem metrics are nominal and access logs show normal HTTP 200 responses, ruling it out as the origin. The propagation chain is: Redis01 saturation/eviction \u2192 cache miss storm \u2192 Tomcat thread pool exhaustion (high ProcessingTime) \u2192 MySQL read amplification \u2192 MG01/MG02 connection saturation \u2192 IG01/IG02 latency elevation \u2192 apache01 alert fires. The trigger is most likely a load test (k6/0.29.0 user-agent visible in access logs across apache01 and Tomcat) that was not rate-limited and overwhelmed Redis01's connection and memory limits."}
groundtruth: The only root cause occurrence time is within 1 minutes (i.e., <=1min) of 2021-03-09 18:08:00
